texto: In the evening, the tarantella dancers will come to the hotel; perhaps they'll dance and sing in the courtyard that is dripping with wistaria blooms and pungent with citrus perfumes. They wear gay costumes, these who sing and dance for us to keep alive the romance of other days; and they are full of that joy in living which seems the gift of these siren shores.
explicacao: Citação de Clara E. texto: I don't know how many years it was before I arrived at a formulated philosophy that the happiest thing to do, always, when visiting an individual or a country, is to admit, by word or manner, how much I'm finding there that my life had lacked hitherto

texto: The great glory of travel, to me, is not just what I see that's new to me in countries visited, but that in almost every one of them I change from an outsider looking in to an insider looking out.
explicacao: Citação de Clara E. Laughlin sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The great glory of travel, to me, is not just what I see that's new to me in countries visited, b…” articula reflexao, travel, looking em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e viagem.
